name: Hakone
description: A popular hot spring resort area featuring views of Mt. Fuji, art museums, and a scenic lake.
reservation: Required well in advance for ryokans.
notes: Purchase the Hakone Free Pass for unlimited rides on local transport.

itineraries.name: Plan A: The Classic Hakone Loop
itineraries.description: Experience all modes of transport and the best views of Mt. Fuji.

steps.activity: Arrive at Hakone-Yumoto and take the scenic Tozan Mountain Railway to Gora.
Transfer to the Hakone Ropeway at Sounzan. Enjoy views over the volcanic Owakudani valley.
Eat black eggs (Kuro-tamago) boiled in the hot springs at Owakudani.
Take the Pirate Ship cruise across Lake Ashi to Motohakone.
Walk the old Cedar Avenue and visit Hakone Shrine.

itineraries.name: Plan B: Art & Relaxation
itineraries.description: A laid-back day focusing on world-class museums and high-end hot springs.

steps.activity: Explore the Hakone Open-Air Museum, featuring Picasso and massive outdoor sculptures.
Lunch at a traditional soba restaurant in Gora.
Visit the Pola Museum of Art, nestled deep in the forest.
Enjoy a premium day-use Onsen (hot spring) with mountain views.
Dinner in Hakone-Yumoto before heading back to Yokohama.

name: Kamakura
description: Coastal town rich in history, known for the Great Buddha, numerous temples, and surfing beaches.
notes: Wear comfortable walking shoes. Enoden line can be very crowded.

itineraries.name: Plan A: Samurai Heritage Tour
itineraries.description: The classic Kamakura route focusing on the most famous shrines and the Great Buddha.

steps.activity: Arrive at Kamakura Station and walk up the iconic Wakamiya Oji approach.
Visit Tsurugaoka Hachimangu, the most important Shinto shrine in Kamakura.
Street food and souvenir shopping along Komachi-dori street.
Take the Enoden train to Hase Station. Visit the giant bronze Great Buddha.
Explore Hase-dera temple and its beautiful hillside gardens and ocean views.

itineraries.name: Plan B: Bamboo Groves & Ocean Breezes
itineraries.description: Avoid the main crowds by exploring eastern Kamakura and the nearby island of Enoshima.

steps.activity: Take a bus to Hokoku-ji Temple. Enjoy matcha tea in the serene bamboo grove.
Lunch at a quiet local cafe in the Jomyoji area.
Ride the Enoden train all the way to Enoshima.
Walk across the bridge to Enoshima Island and explore the shrine and caves.
Watch the sunset over Mt. Fuji from the Enoshima Sea Candle.

name: Kawagoe
description: Known as 'Little Edo', featuring preserved clay-walled warehouse district and a charming candy alley.
parking: Available but traffic can be slow in town.
notes: Famous for sweet potato snacks and traditional unagi.

itineraries.name: Plan A: Little Edo Walk
itineraries.description: Experience the historic warehouse district and famous candy alley.

steps.activity: Arrive at Kawagoe Station and take the retro loop bus.
Stroll the Kurazukuri Zone (Warehouse District) and photograph the Toki no Kane (Time Bell Tower).
Enjoy local sweet potato treats and traditional eel (unagi) for lunch.
Explore Kashiya Yokocho (Candy Alley) for retro Japanese sweets.
Visit Kitain Temple, featuring the only remaining buildings of the original Edo Castle.

itineraries.name: Plan B: Shrines & Culture
itineraries.description: A deeper dive into local traditions, focusing on the famous Hikawa Shrine.

steps.activity: Visit Hikawa Shrine. Walk through the tunnel of wind chimes (in summer) or ema.
Fish for your fortune using a small fishing rod at the shrine.
Lunch at a traditional Japanese restaurant near the Honmaru Goten.
Tour Kawagoe Castle Honmaru Goten, the former samurai lord's residence.
Relax at a retro coffee shop on Taisho-roman Street.

name: Nikko
description: World Heritage site famous for Toshogu Shrine and stunning autumn colors around Lake Chuzenji.
reservation: Required for limited express trains and ryokans.
parking: Extremely crowded during autumn leaves season.
notes: Traffic jams to Okunikko are severe in October.

itineraries.name: Plan A: World Heritage Sanctuaries
itineraries.description: A focused tour of the elaborately decorated shrines and temples.

steps.activity: Arrive in Nikko. Photograph the iconic red Shinkyo Bridge.
Explore Toshogu Shrine, the incredibly ornate mausoleum of Tokugawa Ieyasu.
Lunch featuring local Nikko Yuba (tofu skin) delicacies.
Visit Rinno-ji Temple and the quiet Futarasan Shrine.
Stroll along the Kanmangafuchi Abyss to see the row of stone Jizo statues.

itineraries.name: Plan B: Okunikko Nature Escape
itineraries.description: Skip the shrines and head higher up the mountains for waterfalls and lakes.

steps.activity: Take the bus up the winding Irohazaka Route to Okunikko.
Ride the elevator down to the base of Kegon Falls, one of Japan's top 3 waterfalls.
Lunch overlooking the beautiful Lake Chuzenji.
Hike the wooden boardwalks of the Senjogahara Marshland.
Soak your feet in the public footbaths at Yumoto Onsen before returning.

name: Yokohama
description: Vibrant port city offering romantic night views, large Chinatown, and modern shopping complexes.
reservation: Dinners with night views require booking.
parking: Plenty of paid parking in Minatomirai.
notes: Extremely easy access from central Tokyo.

itineraries.name: Plan A: Minatomirai Classics
itineraries.description: The ultimate waterfront date or family trip hitting all major landmarks.

steps.activity: Design your own custom cup noodles at the Cup Noodle Museum.
Lunch and shopping at the historic Red Brick Warehouse.
Take a ride on the Cosmo Clock 21 Ferris wheel.
Take the Yokohama Air Cabin ropeway across the bay.
Dinner in Yokohama Chinatown, the largest in Japan.

itineraries.name: Plan B: Gardens & History
itineraries.description: A quieter side of Yokohama, focusing on classic Japanese gardens and port history.

steps.activity: Explore Sankeien Garden, featuring historic buildings relocated from across Japan.
Lunch in the elegant Motomachi Shopping Street.
Stroll up to the Yamate Bluffs to see the historic Western-style diplomatic houses.
Walk through Yamashita Park and tour the NYK Hikawa Maru ocean liner.
Cocktails at a high-rise bar in the Landmark Tower.

name: Atami
description: A retro-chic seaside hot spring town famous for its beaches, fresh seafood, and frequent fireworks.
reservation: Essential during summer and fireworks days.
notes: Check the schedule for Atami's year-round fireworks displays!

itineraries.name: Plan A: Seaside & Art
itineraries.description: A mix of ocean views and world-class museums.

steps.activity: Arrive in Atami. Take the bus to the spectacular MOA Museum of Art.
Lunch near Atami Sun Beach, enjoying fresh local seafood.
Take the ropeway up to Atami Castle for panoramic bay views.
Explore the retro shopping streets near the station and eat onsen manju.
Take a quick dip in a public bath before taking the Shinkansen home.

itineraries.name: Plan B: Retro Atami Vibe
itineraries.description: Focus on the nostalgic Showa-era atmosphere of this classic resort town.

steps.activity: Visit Kiunkaku, a gorgeous former ryokan loved by Japanese literary giants.
Eat a classic Japanese curry or omurice at a retro Kissaten (coffee shop).
Visit Kinomiya Shrine to see the 2,000-year-old sacred camphor tree.
Stroll along the oceanfront and find a quiet day-use hot spring.
Dinner at a local izakaya in the old town.

name: Boso Peninsula
description: Scenic coastal drives, fresh seafood, and nature spots perfect for a day trip by car.

itineraries.name: Plan A: Mountain & Buddha
itineraries.description: A hiking and cultural adventure on Mt. Nokogiri.

steps.activity: Arrive at Hamakanaya and take the Mt. Nokogiri Ropeway up the mountain.
Hike to the Jigoku Nozoki (Hell's Peek) viewpoint.
Descend into the quarry to see the massive Nihon-ji Daibutsu (Great Buddha).
Late lunch of fresh caught seafood near the ferry terminal.
Relax at the Baya-no-yu oceanfront hot spring.

itineraries.name: Plan B: Coastline Road Trip
itineraries.description: Best done by car. Exploring the scenic drives of Chiba.

steps.activity: Drive the Tokyo Bay Aqua-Line and stop at Umihotaru PA.
Visit Mother Farm to see the animals and flower fields.
Drive down to Katsuura or Kamogawa for an incredible seafood lunch.
Explore the Kamogawa Sea World or take a coastal drive to the lighthouse.
Watch the sunset over the Pacific Ocean.

name: Matsumoto
description: Historic mountain city famous for Matsumoto Castle, one of Japan's original historic castles.
notes: Long drive but rewarding

itineraries.name: Plan A: The Black Castle
itineraries.description: Focus on the historic samurai history of Matsumoto.

steps.activity: Arrive in Matsumoto. Head straight to Matsumoto Castle, one of Japan's oldest.
Have Shinshu Soba for lunch, a local specialty.
Stroll along Nakamachi-dori, known for its historic black-and-white storehouses.
Wander Nawate-dori (Frog Street) for quirky shops and street food.
Visit a local sake brewery for tasting before dinner.

itineraries.name: Plan B: Art & Alps
itineraries.description: A cultural day featuring famous contemporary art and mountain views.

steps.activity: Visit the Matsumoto City Museum of Art to see the permanent Yayoi Kusama exhibition.
Lunch at a trendy cafe near the museum district.
Take a short bus or taxi ride up to Alps Park.
Enjoy panoramic views of the Japan Alps and explore the nature trails.
Head back to town for a cozy dinner.

name: Harry Potter Studio
description: Warner Bros. Studio Tour Tokyo - The Making of Harry Potter. A magical indoor experience.
notes: Book ahead

itineraries.name: Plan A: The Ultimate Fan Experience
itineraries.description: A comprehensive walk-through prioritizing all major sets and photo ops.

steps.activity: Arrive early and enter the Great Hall.
Explore the Hogwarts Express and Platform 9 ¾.
Drink Butterbeer in the Backlot Cafe.
Walk through Diagon Alley and the Ministry of Magic sets.
Spend an hour in the massive studio gift shop.

itineraries.name: Plan B: The Filmmaker's Focus
itineraries.description: Take your time studying the props, costumes, and behind-the-scenes magic.

steps.activity: Focus heavily on the Art Department and Costume exhibits.
Participate in the interactive green-screen broomstick flying.
Lunch at the Food Hall.
Study the creature effects and animatronics in the Creature Shop.
Examine the incredibly detailed Hogwarts Castle miniature.

name: Karuizawa
description: An upscale mountain resort town famous for summer retreats, outlet shopping, and nature.
notes: Cool escape from Tokyo summer

itineraries.name: Plan A: The Classic Resort
itineraries.description: Shopping, dining, and scenic walks in the famous mountain resort.

steps.activity: Rent a bicycle near Karuizawa Station.
Cycle to Kumoba Pond (Swan Lake) for a beautiful nature walk.
Explore the Kyu-Karuizawa Ginza street. Eat local breads and jams.
Cycle to the historic Shaw Memorial Church and the deep forest.
Finish the day shopping at the massive Prince Shopping Plaza outlet mall.

itineraries.name: Plan B: Art & Architecture
itineraries.description: Discover the elegant modern architecture and waterfalls of the area.

steps.activity: Take a bus to the breathtaking Shiraito Falls.
Head to Harunire Terrace for a stylish lunch in the forest.
Walk to the Stone Church and the Karuizawa Kogen Church.
Visit the Hiroshi Senju Museum, a stunning piece of modern architecture.
Relax at the Tombo-no-yu hot springs.

name: Utsunomiya & Oya
description: Famous for its massive underground Oya stone quarry and delicious local gyoza.
notes: Oya museum is very cool in summer

itineraries.name: Plan A: Stone & Gyoza
itineraries.description: The perfect mix of exploring massive underground caves and eating famous local food.

steps.activity: Take a bus from Utsunomiya Station to the Oya History Museum.
Explore the massive, atmospheric underground stone quarry.
Visit the nearby Oya-ji Temple and the giant Heiwa Kannon statue.
Head back to central Utsunomiya.
Go 'Gyoza Hopping' – try 3 different famous gyoza shops for a late lunch/dinner.

itineraries.name: Plan B: Scenic Cycling
itineraries.description: Rent a bike and explore the rural beauty surrounding the stone town.

steps.activity: Rent a bicycle near Utsunomiya Station.
Cycle through the scenic countryside towards the Oya district.
Lunch at a stylish cafe built into an old stone warehouse.
Explore the ancient cliff carvings at Oya-ji Temple.
Cycle back, drop off the bike, and reward yourself with gyoza.

name: DisneySea
description: Unique ocean-themed Disney park with spectacular shows, rides, and adult-friendly atmosphere.
notes: Avoid hottest days

itineraries.name: Plan A: The Adrenaline Rush
itineraries.description: Optimize your day to hit the biggest thrill rides.

steps.activity: Arrive at rope drop. Head straight to Journey to the Center of the Earth.
Ride Indiana Jones Adventure and Raging Spirits.
Grab a quick bite (smoked turkey leg or spicy chicken) while walking.
Use Premier Access for Tower of Terror or Soaring: Fantastic Flight.
Secure a spot for the nighttime spectacular show on the Mediterranean Harbor.

itineraries.name: Plan B: Romantic & Immersive
itineraries.description: A slow-paced day focusing on atmosphere, shows, and fine dining.

steps.activity: Arrive and take a relaxing ride on the Venetian Gondolas.
Watch the incredible Big Band Beat jazz show.
Enjoy a sit-down lunch at the Magellan's fine dining restaurant.
Explore the interactive Fortress Explorations and walk through Arabian Coast.
Ride the Transit Steamer Line at sunset for beautiful views.

name: Mt. Tsukuba
description: A beautiful twin-peaked mountain famous for its cable car, shrines, and sweeping views of the Kanto plain.
notes: Use ropeway to save time

itineraries.name: Plan A: The Twin Peak Hike
itineraries.description: A moderately challenging hike to the summits for incredible Kanto Plain views.

steps.activity: Take the Tsukuba Express, then a bus to the Tsukubasan Shrine.
Take the Cable Car up to Miyukigahara (the plateau between peaks).
Hike to the summit of Mt. Nantai, then traverse to Mt. Nyotai.
Enjoy a bowl of Tsukuba Udon at the mountaintop restaurant.
Take the Ropeway down to the Tsutsujigaoka station.

itineraries.name: Plan B: Science & Shrines
itineraries.description: Mix the natural beauty of the shrine with Tsukuba's famous Space Center.

steps.activity: Visit the JAXA Tsukuba Space Center. See the rocket displays.
Lunch in Tsukuba city center.
Take the bus to Tsukubasan Shrine and explore the ancient grounds.
Take the cable car up for a quick panoramic view without the hiking.
Relax at an onsen near the base of the mountain.

name: Omiya Railway Museum
description: Massive indoor museum dedicated to Japan's railway history, featuring real historic trains.
notes: Excellent hot-day option

itineraries.name: Plan A: The Train Otaku
itineraries.description: A deep dive into the history and mechanics of Japan's legendary railway system.

steps.activity: Arrive at the museum and head straight to the massive Rolling Stock Station.
Book and experience the Steam Locomotive or Shinkansen Simulator.
Eat an authentic 'Ekiben' (station bento) inside a retired train car.
Watch the massive Railway Diorama show.
Explore the History Zone and learn about the evolution of the Shinkansen.

itineraries.name: Plan B: Family Fun Day
itineraries.description: A relaxed pace focused on interactive exhibits perfect for kids.

steps.activity: Arrive and immediately head to the Mini Driving Train area.
Lunch at the Kids Cafe, which has train-themed meals.
Let the kids play in the Teppaku Playroom and outdoor park.
Take family photos with the historic steam engines.
Buy exclusive train toys at the museum shop before leaving.

name: Joypolis Odaiba
description: Sega's massive indoor amusement park with VR attractions, roller coasters, and arcade games.
notes: Combine with Odaiba

itineraries.name: Plan A: Thrills & Coasters
itineraries.description: Tackle the most intense, adrenaline-pumping rides the indoor park has to offer.

steps.activity: Arrive at Decks Tokyo Beach and head into Joypolis.
Ride the Gekion Live Coaster, a rhythm game combined with a rollercoaster.
Compete against friends on the Halfpipe Tokyo spinning ride.
Quick lunch at the Frame Cafe inside the park overlooking the bay.
Ride the Transformers 360-degree spinning simulator.

itineraries.name: Plan B: Horror & Virtual Reality
itineraries.description: Focus on the immersive walk-through attractions and cutting-edge VR.

steps.activity: Start with the Zero Latency VR free-roaming experience (book in advance!).
Grab lunch at a restaurant in Odaiba outside the park.
Experience the Room of the Living Doll 3D sound horror attraction.
Play the interactive Phoenix Wright: Ace Attorney investigation game.
Watch the main stage projection mapping show before leaving.

name: Tokyo Disneyland
description: The classic Disney theme park experience with iconic parades and attractions.
notes: Book tickets online up to 2 months in advance.

name: Ghibli Museum
description: A whimsical museum dedicated to the art and animation of Studio Ghibli.
reservation: Tickets sell out within hours. Book on the 10th of the previous month.
parking: Not recommended, take the train to Mitaka.
notes: No photography allowed inside the museum.

name: Yomiuriland
description: A classic amusement park in Tokyo known for thrilling roller coasters and spectacular winter illuminations.
notes: Winter Jewellumination is incredibly famous and romantic.

name: teamLab Planets
description: An immersive digital art museum where you walk barefoot through massive, interactive water and light installations.
notes: You will walk barefoot and water goes up to your knees. Wear shorts or pants that can be rolled up.

name: Ginza
description: Tokyo's most prestigious shopping district, blending high-end fashion, traditional crafts, and world-class dining.
notes: Main street is closed to traffic on weekends (Pedestrian Paradise).

itineraries.name: Plan A: Cultured Urban Stroll
itineraries.description: Discover high-quality Japanese craftsmanship, from precision watches to perfect paper.

steps.activity: Explore the Seiko Museum Ginza to learn about the history of Japanese timepieces.
Lunch at a traditional tempura or sushi restaurant.
Spend an hour wandering the 12 floors of Itoya, the ultimate stationery store.
Relax at a classic kissaten (retro cafe) with hand-drip coffee.
Walk the Chuo-dori pedestrian paradise (weekends only).

name: Enoshima
description: A delightfully pleasant island just off the coast, full of shrines, coastal cliffs, and sweeping views of Mt. Fuji.
notes: Try the local specialty: Shirasu (whitebait) bowl.

itineraries.name: Plan A: Ocean Breeze & Shrines
itineraries.description: Hike over the island from the bustling shrine approach to the dramatic southern cliffs.

steps.activity: Walk across the bridge to Enoshima. Grab a giant senbei cracker on the approach.
Visit Enoshima Shrine. Take the escalators up if you want to skip the stairs.
Eat fresh Shirasu-don for lunch.
Visit the Samuel Cocking Garden and Sea Candle for panoramic views.
Walk down to the Iwaya Caves on the rocky southern coast.

name: Akihabara (Chiyoda)
description: The epicenter of anime, gaming, and electronics culture in Japan. Bustling with themed cafes and arcades.
notes: Sunday afternoons have a pedestrian paradise on Chuo-dori.

name: Imperial Palace (Chiyoda)
description: The primary residence of the Emperor of Japan. Surrounded by moats, stone walls, and beautiful gardens.
notes: East Gardens are closed on Mondays and Fridays.

name: Odaiba (Minato)
description: A high-tech entertainment hub on an artificial island in Tokyo Bay, featuring shopping malls, beaches, and a giant Gundam.
parking: Plenty of paid parking in malls.
notes: Take the Yurikamome line for an automated scenic ride across the bay.

name: Ikebukuro (Toshima)
description: A massive commercial district in northwestern Tokyo with huge department stores, anime culture, and Sunshine City.
notes: Excellent choice for rainy days due to Sunshine City and massive underground networks.

name: Ueno (Taito)
description: A cultural hub featuring a massive park, Japan's oldest zoo, major national museums, and the bustling Ameyoko market.
notes: In spring, the park is one of Tokyo's most crowded cherry blossom spots.

name: Shinjuku
description: Tokyo's vibrant core. Neon lights, skyscrapers, endless shopping, and the serene Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden.
parking: Available in major towers, very expensive.
notes: Shinjuku Station is a maze. Meet at the East Exit Studio Alta or South Exit.

name: Shibuya
description: Youth culture, trendy fashion, and the iconic Shibuya Scramble Crossing. Constantly evolving with new high-rises like Shibuya Sky.
notes: Book Shibuya Sky tickets for sunset at least 2 weeks in advance.

name: Edogawa
description: Known for Kasai Rinkai Park on Tokyo Bay, featuring a giant Ferris wheel, aquarium, and peaceful riverbank cycling.
parking: Ample parking at Kasai Rinkai Park.
notes: Great sunset views of Mt. Fuji and Tokyo Disneyland from the park.

name: Asakusa (Taito)
description: Tokyo's traditional heart, built around Senso-ji Temple. Nakamise shopping street offers snacks and souvenirs.
notes: Go early morning (before 9 AM) to photograph Kaminarimon without crowds.

name: Akasaka (Minato)
description: An upscale commercial and residential district with excellent dining, high-end hotels, and Hie Shrine.
notes: Hie Shrine has a beautiful torii gate tunnel similar to Fushimi Inari in Kyoto.

name: Meguro
description: Famous worldwide for the Meguro River cherry blossoms. Features trendy cafes, boutiques, and a relaxed atmosphere.
reservation: Not required, but cafes fill up.
notes: Spring cherry blossoms are incredibly beautiful but completely packed with people.
